name: obsidian-helper
description: Obsidian intelligent note-taking assistant. Activates when users mention obsidian, diary, notes, knowledge base, capture, or review. Provides daily journaling, knowledge capture, and periodic review features.
capabilities: Create and manage daily notes with automatic inheritance of unfinished tasks from previous day
Capture knowledge by searching existing notes and intelligently appending to relevant documents
Generate weekly and monthly review reports by analyzing note content and task completion
Guide users through Obsidian MCP setup with step-by-step configuration instructions
Follow structured vault organization with PARA and Zettelkasten methodologies
Support Chinese and English interfaces for global users
author: Claude Code
securitySummary: This skill contains documentation and instructional content only. All 177 static analysis findings are false positives: the 'external_commands' are markdown code blocks showing example commands for users, 'network' findings are documentation URLs, 'filesystem' references are configuration paths in documentation, and 'env_access' mentions are examples for API key placement. No actual code execution, network requests, or file system access occurs within the skill itself.
valueStatement: Manage your Obsidian knowledge base efficiently with automated daily journaling, intelligent knowledge capture, and periodic review generation. Integrates seamlessly with Obsidian MCP for hands-free note management.
limitations: Requires Obsidian desktop application with Local REST API plugin installed and running
Depends on mcp-obsidian package being configured in Claude Code MCP settings
Cannot execute Obsidian commands directly - relies on user having proper MCP configuration
Knowledge capture requires existing notes for matching - new topics create new notes

useCases.targetUser: Professionals managing daily tasks and meeting notes
useCases.title: Daily Knowledge Worker
useCases.description: Start each day with automated journal setup that carries over unfinished tasks and provides structured templates for notes and reflections.

useCases.targetUser: Academic researchers and graduate students building literature reviews
useCases.title: Researcher or Student
useCases.description: Capture research insights, lecture notes, and ideas quickly while the system organizes them into a connected knowledge graph.

useCases.targetUser: Lifelong learners and content creators maintaining personal wikis
useCases.title: Personal Knowledge Base Builder
useCases.description: Implement PARA and Zettelkasten methodologies with automated periodic reviews to surface insights and track progress.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Start - Daily Note
promptTemplates.prompt: Create today's daily note in Obsidian, carrying over any unfinished tasks from yesterday.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting your workday and want to quickly set up your journal

promptTemplates.title: Knowledge Capture
promptTemplates.prompt: Capture this idea to my Obsidian vault: RESTful API design should use nouns for resources and verbs only for HTTP methods.
promptTemplates.scenario: You have a sudden insight during a meeting or while reading documentation

promptTemplates.title: Weekly Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a weekly review for the past 7 days, summarizing completed tasks, active projects, and key insights.
promptTemplates.scenario: End of week reflection to track progress and plan next week

promptTemplates.title: Setup and Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me configure Obsidian integration with Claude Code. I need step-by-step instructions for my setup.
promptTemplates.scenario: First-time user needing to establish the MCP connection between Claude and Obsidian

bestPractices: Always review AI-generated content in the 00_Inbox/AI folder before moving to permanent locations
Use consistent properties (type, tags, status) across notes to enable powerful Dataview queries
Run periodic reviews weekly to maintain awareness of progress and emerging knowledge patterns
antiPatterns: Do not skip the MCP connection check - verify Obsidian is running before executing commands
Avoid creating notes outside the designated folders without explicit user confirmation
Do not rewrite entire notes - use append and patch operations to preserve existing content

faq.question: What do I need to install before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need Obsidian desktop app, the Local REST API plugin installed in Obsidian, and the mcp-obsidian package configured in your Claude Code MCP settings. The skill will guide you through setup on first use.

faq.question: Where are my notes stored?
faq.answer: Notes are stored in your Obsidian vault. New content from AI goes to 00_Inbox/AI/ by default, then you can move it to appropriate folders like Daily Notes, Projects, or Resources.

faq.question: Can this skill work without the Obsidian app running?
faq.answer: No, the Local REST API plugin must be active in a running Obsidian instance for the MCP tools to communicate with your vault.

faq.question: How does the daily note inheritance work?
faq.answer: The skill reads yesterday's daily note, extracts all unchecked todo items (lines starting with - [ ]), and adds them to today's note under a 'From Yesterday' section.

faq.question: What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports both Chinese and English interfaces. Configuration guides and user-facing messages adapt to your language preference.

faq.question: Can I customize the note templates?
faq.answer: Yes, create a _config/obsidian-helper.md file in your vault to customize folder locations, date formats, and other settings that the skill respects.
